name: Turkey Piccata
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the flour with salt and freshly ground pepper.
2. Dredge each turkey cutlet in the seasoned flour, coating evenly on both sides.
3. Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet over medium heat until it is hot but not smoking.
4. Add 8 tablespoons of unsalted butter to the skillet and wait for the foam to subside.
5. Carefully add the turkey cutlets to the skillet and sauté several minutes on each side, depending on the size and thickness of the cutlets, until they are golden brown and cooked through.
6. Remove the turkey cutlets from the skillet with a spatula and keep them warm.
7. To make the sauce, melt 1 tablespoon of butter in the hot skillet.
8. Add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and reduce the mixture slightly over high heat, stirring occasionally, until it thickens to your desired consistency.
9. Season the sauce to taste with salt and pepper.
10. Pour the sauce over the warm turkey cutlets and garnish with minced parsley before serving.
Note: Ensure all steps are executed carefully to prevent any burns or splatters from the hot butter and lemon juice in the skillet.
